medications.drug_name: Oral analgesia or any oral intake at home
medications.indication_rationale: The patient must be kept nil by mouth in anticipation of emergency surgery; giving oral analgesia or fluids at home is inappropriate and delays definitive care. Pain relief is managed in hospital.

medications.drug_name: Additional dose of the patient's antihypertensive
medications.indication_rationale: The blood pressure is already borderline-low with presyncope; taking an extra antihypertensive dose at home risks worsening hypoperfusion. Any blood-pressure control in this setting is a titrated in-hospital decision.
medications.deprescribing_note: Withhold further doses of regular antihypertensive medication until assessed in hospital, given borderline blood pressure and presyncope.

medications.indication_rationale: Nitrates would drop the blood pressure further and are not indicated; misapplying a cardiac-chest-pain reflex here would be harmful.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Remain strictly supine and still; do not stand, walk or strain until the ambulance arrives.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Standing precipitates presyncope and movement/straining increases aortic wall stress and rupture risk.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Practise slow, controlled breathing to keep calm while awaiting paramedics.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Reducing anxiety-driven tachycardia and blood-pressure surges lowers aortic shear stress during transport.

patient_education_points.point: The main blood vessel in the abdomen may be dangerously stretched and needs an urgent hospital scan and specialist review — this is an emergency, not something to watch at home.
Do not eat or drink anything, in case an emergency operation is needed.
Stay lying flat and still; do not stand, walk, or try to travel to hospital yourself — wait for the ambulance.
If sudden severe tearing pain, fainting, or heavy sweating develops, this is a life-threatening emergency — stay on the line with 000.

follow_up_plan.interval: immediate emergency ambulance transfer now; vascular surgical review on arrival; routine GP review after discharge
follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Haemodynamic stability, CT-angiography findings, aortic diameter and rupture status, and surgical candidacy in hospital.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Any sudden severe tearing pain, collapse, loss of consciousness or profound sweating before the ambulance arrives — call 000 back immediately.

must_include_items: Recognise this as a time-critical vascular emergency (symptomatic/expanding abdominal aortic aneurysm) rather than a benign mimic
Call 000 for an emergency ambulance without delay
Instruct the patient to remain strictly supine and still and NOT to stand, walk or self-transport
Advise nil by mouth in anticipation of emergency surgery
Arrange immediate emergency-department transfer for CT angiography and vascular surgical review
acceptable_alternatives: Explicitly naming suspected aortic rupture/dissection as the concern while escalating to 000 is acceptable, provided the ambulance and positioning advice are given.
errors_of_omission: Failing to escalate to an emergency ambulance (000)
Failing to advise the patient to stay supine and still
Failing to advise nil by mouth
errors_of_commission: Attributing the presentation to musculoskeletal back pain, renal colic or diverticulitis and advising analgesia or a routine GP review
Advising the patient to drive or be driven to hospital instead of calling an ambulance
Recommending an extra antihypertensive dose, nitrates or other blood-pressure-lowering medication at home
Recommending next-day or outpatient imaging instead of emergency transfer
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: A pass REQUIRES recognising the emergency and escalating to 000 with supine/nil-by-mouth advice. Under-triage to GP/analgesia/self-transport is an automatic FAIL regardless of other content.
